other_observers: None; Yes. Brandon Percival later observed this bird  9-12-07
specific_location: Orlando Reservoir about 1 mile east of I-25 south of exit 64.
description: This bird was a plump dowitcher sized and shaped bird, but having a much shorter bill, a bit more than half the length of nearby L-b Dowitchers. Its legs also appeared proportionately shorter than Dowitchers.  Bird was overall gray above and whitish below with a whitish supercilium and somewhat peaked crown. The smooth unscaled gray upperparts suggested an adult bird. Whitish underparts became a bit grayer on the breast with diffuse dusky speckling on lower breast and obscure dusky barring on flanks. I did not see bird fly, but at rest the primaries extended about even with the tail giving the bird a somewhat long-winged appearance.  Bare parts: Bill & eye were black; legs were grayish olive.Behaviors: Bird did very little during observation time mostly standing in a stationary position and occasionally probing to feed.Call: None givenPlumage: Winter
